prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Arrr, ye scurvy landlubber! Ye be wonderin’ why the sky be blue, eh? Well, let ol’ Cap’n tell ye. Ye see, the sun be sendin’ down its light, and that there light be made o’ all colors o’ the rainbow, like a chest o’ treasure spillin’ its riches. But the sky, she be a tricky lass—she don’t let all them colors pass through easy. Nay, she be scatterin’ the shorter, bluer waves every which way, while the longer, redder ones slip through like a ghost ship in the night. So, when ye look up, all ye see be that fine, briny blue, like the deep waters we sail upon. Now, enough jawin’—back to work, ye bilge rat! Yo ho!
